design_element_categories.label: Architectural Elements
design_element_categories.description: Includes major architectural elements like walls, doors, windows, skylights, etc.

design_element_categories.label: Furnishings & Finishes
design_element_categories.description: Includes furniture and  finish materials (including flooring and ceiling materials, wall protection, etc.), equipment, furnishings, accessories, casework, and decoration

design_element_categories.label: MEP & Building Systems
design_element_categories.description: Includes mechanical, electrical, and plumbing elements and systems, acoustics, headwalls, lighting, etc.

design_element_categories.label: Technology & Other Elements
design_element_categories.description: Includes IT and communication, security and patient monitoring, and entertainment systems and devices

items.text: What is the strategic vision for the hospital/health system?
What are the main drivers/motivators for the ED redesign (for renovation)/ design (new construction) project?
Is this project a renovation or new construction?
Does the ED serve pediatric patients, adult patients, or both?
What is the anticipated total annual patient volume for this ED?
What is the anticipated total annual mental and behavioral health patient volume for this ED?
Will the ED serve any prisoners/penitentiary patients?
What is the anticipated  proportion of uninsured patients in the ED?
Approximately what percentage of patients that will be seen in this ED (for all visit reasons) are:
Based on the ED's annual patient data (last 5 years), please share the annual patient volumes for each these categories.
iv. Substance Use and Addictive Disorder
Based on the ED's annual patient data (last 5 years), please share the weekly patient volumes for each these categories.
Do you anticipate any seasonal variations in the weekly patient volumes? If yes, please share your anticipated weekly patient volumes during peak season.
Based on the ED's annual patient data (last 5 years), what is the typical length of stay for MBH patients in the ED?
Do you anticipate implementing any of the following care models:
b.  Dedicated Mental/Behavioral Health unit for adult/pediatric patients
c. Separate care pathway for pediatric Mental/Behavioral Health patients
d. Boarding pediatric MBH patients in in-patient rooms on medical floors
What are the discharge options for:
a.  Inpatient psychiatric care within your hospital
b.  Inpatient psychiatric care in your community?
c. Outpatient psychiatric care in your community?
Based on your anticipated patient volume, how many total exam rooms would you like to have in the ED?
Based on your anticipated MBH VOLUME, approximately what percentage of ED patient rooms will be utilized as:
a. Only mental and behavioral health needs
c. Both medical and mental/behavioral health needs
Do you anticipate having a locked ED Unit?

outcome_prioritization.title: Design Objectives Worksheet

outcomes.description: Preventing self-harm involves implementing strategies to identify at-risk individuals, provide timely support, and reduce factors that contribute to self-injurious behaviors.
Preventing elopement involves implementing strategies and interventions to reduce the risk of individuals from leaving a safe environment without supervision.
Ensure rapid identification and intervention in emergencies and (self) harm incidents to minimize risk, providing immediate support from healthcare staff.
Perception of Safety refers to individuals’ feelings and beliefs about how safe they are in a given environment.
Improve the speed and effectiveness of interventions when patients initiate harm to others or to the environment.
Reduce patient-initiated harm aims to minimize incidents where patients cause harm to themselves, staff, or others within the healthcare setting.
Reduce the frequency and necessity of using physical or chemical restraints.
Reduced exposure to triggering content aims to minimize an individual's contact with situations, media or people that may provoke distressing emotions or reactions.
Provide visual access to the patient, improving the ability of healthcare providers to access, track, and monitor patient information in real time.
Implement strategies and tools to minimize manual and mental, reducing the overall workload and task demmands for staff.

outcomes.outcome: Reduce ED waiting times and/or length of stay
outcomes.description: Implement targeted process improvements to reduce emergency department waiting times and overall length of stay.

outcomes.outcome: Adequate care for co-occurring med/psych conditions
outcomes.description: Ensure that individuals receive appropriate assessment, treatment, and ongoing management for both medical and psychiatric conditions.
Streamline the movement of patients through the ED during various stages of care, ensuring timely admissions, transfers, and discharges.

outcomes.outcome: Provision of appropriate psych care in the ED
outcomes.description: Ensure that patients presenting to the ED with MBH concerns receive timely assessment, stabilization, and access to appropriate psychiatric interventions.
Foster a collaborative environment to ensure team members work effectively together towards shared goals.
Ensure that the medical care teams provide timely and effective responses to urgent situations and codes.
Optimizing communication channels and processes to ensure that relevant data and updates are efficiently shared among care team members.
Provide comprehensive support to allow staff and resident training in the ED
Provide clarity of information in interactions with family members, minimizing misunderstandings.

objectives.objective: Improve Overall Comfort for All Users

outcomes.description: Enhance the satisfaction and emotional well-being of patients and their families by addressing their needs, preferences, and concerns.
Enhance the physical and physiological well-being of patients and their families.
Reduces anxiety by promoting relaxation, calming the mind, and decreasing feelings of worry or nervousness.
Minimize the number of people in a given space at one time, to reduce the discomfort and stress associated with the perception of being crowded
Enhanced staff satisfaction focuses on improving the emotional well-being, engagement, and morale of employees
Encourage and support family involvement in the patient’s care and empower to participate in decision-making and care activities.
Establishes a positive, respectful, and empathetic environment to the patient and family members need
Ensure that all patient information is protected from unauthorized access, disclosure, or misuse by following established privacy policies and practices such as HIPAA.
Reduce the likelihood that of patients to be exposed to other patients or the general public after being admitted to ED.
Reduces agitation and boredom, minimizing restlessness and disruptive behaviors.
Foster an environment where individuals feel respected, valued, and empowered, promoting self-worth and personal pride in all interactions and activities.
Enhance the ability to manage emotions and establish healthy sleep routines while in the ED

outcome_prioritization.note: User rates each outcome's Importance. Objective value = AVERAGE of its outcomes' importance scores (0-5). Objectives then ranked high->low into priority order (SORTBY).

guideline_prioritization.title: Design Guidelines Prioritization Worksheet

guidelines.guideline: 1. Maximize staff's situational awareness of the patient
2. Reduce opportunities for the patient to cause harm
related_objectives: Improve Overall Comfort for all users

guidelines.guideline: 3. Enhance sense of security for the patient and caregiver
related_objectives: Improve Overall Comfort for all users
Improve Overall Comfort for all users

guidelines.guideline: 5. Improve safety and security for staff
6. Provide flexible and adaptable work environments

related_objectives: Improve Overall Comfort for all users
Improve Overall Comfort for all users

guidelines.guideline: 9. Provide opportunities for engaging patients and care partners
related_objectives: Improve Overall Comfort for all users

guidelines.guideline: 10. Support patient and family dignity and comfort
related_objectives: Improve Overall Comfort for all users

guidelines.guideline: 11. Create opportunities for auditory and visual privacy
related_objectives: Improve Overall Comfort for all users

guidelines.guideline: 12. Provide patient choice and control over the environment
related_objectives: Improve Overall Comfort for all users
Improve Overall Comfort for all users

guidelines.guideline: 14.Support clarity and anticipation of the care path and steps in the healthcare experience.
related_objectives: Improve Overall Comfort for all users

guidelines.guideline: 15. Provide timely access to psychiatric care
related_objectives: Improve Overall Comfort for all users

guideline_prioritization.note: Each guideline's derived importance = ROUND( sum over related objectives of that objective's priority-order score (guideline_priority_scale) / count_of_related_objectives ), mapped back to an importance label. User may override via 'Review Importance'.

policy_process.note: Per row: open-ended design response. Score = SUMPRODUCT of related outcomes' importance scores; Round; map to importance label. Discussion/prioritization aid.

design_alignment.title: Design Alignment Tool

strategies.guideline: 1. Maximize staff's situational awareness of the patient
strategies.strategy: 1.1The patient's head and limbs can be seen from the hallway when they are in the bed/chair.

strategies.guideline: 1. Maximize staff's situational awareness of the patient
strategies.strategy: 1.2. There are no blind spots or hiding spots inside the room when looking from the hallway/doorway.

strategies.guideline: 1. Maximize staff's situational awareness of the patient
strategies.strategy: 1.4. Surveillance cameras provide direct visual access to the room's door(s) and any other access points.

strategies.guideline: 1. Maximize staff's situational awareness of the patient
strategies.strategy: 1.5 Surveillance cameras are discretely integrated into the design.

strategies.guideline: 1. Maximize staff's situational awareness of the patient
strategies.strategy: 1.6. Doors do not lock from the inside. If present, any locks are on the outside (department/hallway side) of the door.

strategies.guideline: 1. Maximize staff's situational awareness of the patient
strategies.strategy: 1.7. Space for an observer (chair, alcove, etc.) is positioned so that it has direct visual access to the room's door(s) and any other access points.

strategies.guideline: 2. Reduce opportunities for the patient to cause harm
strategies.strategy: 2.1. If located in the room, the sink and all its parts (handles, faucets, etc.) are ligature-minimized and tamper-resistant.

strategies.guideline: 2. Reduce opportunities for the patient to cause harm
strategies.strategy: 2.2. If located in the room, the computer workstation and all its parts (mounting, monitor, cords, etc.) are removable and/or ligature-minimized and tamper-resistant.

strategies.guideline: 2. Reduce opportunities for the patient to cause harm
strategies.strategy: 2.3. Any televisions and/or entertainment devices in the room and all their parts ( screens, controls, etc.) are ligature-minimized and tamper-resistant.

strategies.guideline: 2. Reduce opportunities for the patient to cause harm
strategies.strategy: 2.4. Any emergency call buttons or other communication devices in the room are ligature-minimized and tamper-resistant.

strategies.guideline: 2. Reduce opportunities for the patient to cause harm
strategies.strategy: 2.5. Any dispensers or other accessories present in the room are ligature-minimized and tamper-resistant.

strategies.guideline: 2. Reduce opportunities for the patient to cause harm
strategies.strategy: 2.6. Every element in the room that could pose a ligature or weaponization risk can be made inaccessible to patients.

strategies.guideline: 2. Reduce opportunities for the patient to cause harm
strategies.strategy: 2.7. The patient does not have any access to slats, cords, mechanical components or any other parts of window treatments that could pose a weaponization, harm, or ligature risk.

strategies.guideline: 2. Reduce opportunities for the patient to cause harm
strategies.strategy: 2.8. Furniture, fixtures, and surfaces do not have sharp edges, protruding elements, joints, or gaps that could be used for weaponization, harm, or ligature.

strategies.guideline: 2. Reduce opportunities for the patient to cause harm
strategies.strategy: 2.9. Casework and hardware are ligature-minimized and tamper-resistant.

strategies.guideline: 2. Reduce opportunities for the patient to cause harm
strategies.strategy: 2.10. Casework and furnishings are designed to prevent patients from climbing.

strategies.guideline: 2. Reduce opportunities for the patient to cause harm
strategies.strategy: 2.11. All electrical and mechanical fixtures (including switches, receptacles, grilles, etc.) are ligature-minimized and tamper-resistant.

strategies.guideline: 2. Reduce opportunities for the patient to cause harm
strategies.strategy: 2.12. The ceiling is ligature-minimized and tamper-resistant.

strategies.guideline: 3. Enhance sense of security for the patient and caregiver
strategies.strategy: 3.1. The patient bed/chair is oriented away from direct view of the hallway to minimize the patient's exposure to ED activities that may be over-stimulating or distressing.

strategies.guideline: 3. Enhance sense of security for the patient and caregiver
strategies.strategy: 3.2 The room's design minimizes direct visual exposure of the patient from the hallway.

strategies.guideline: 3. Enhance sense of security for the patient and caregiver
strategies.strategy: 3.3. The emergency call button and/or communication device is easily accessible from the patient bed/chair.

strategies.guideline: 3. Enhance sense of security for the patient and caregiver
strategies.strategy: 3.4.The emergency call button and/or communication device is easily accessible to the care partner(s).

strategies.guideline: 3. Enhance sense of security for the patient and caregiver
strategies.strategy: 3.5. Staff can supervise and/or curate entertainment and media options accessible to the patient

strategies.guideline: 4. Optimize staff workflow
strategies.strategy: 4.1. The sink (either inside or outside the room) is immediately visible and accessible to the room's entrance.

strategies.guideline: 4. Optimize staff workflow
strategies.strategy: 4.2. Hand sanitizer, gloves, and other PPE are immediately accessible and visible to the room's entrance.

strategies.guideline: 4. Optimize staff workflow
strategies.strategy: 4.3. There is a dedicated surface near the sink for placing items during handwashing.

strategies.guideline: 4. Optimize staff workflow
strategies.strategy: 4.4. All fixtures, finishes, and furnishings are easy to clean.

strategies.guideline: 4. Optimize staff workflow
strategies.strategy: 4.5. The waste bin is easy for staff members to access while being out of the patient's immediate reach.

strategies.guideline: 4. Optimize staff workflow
strategies.strategy: 4.6. Any adjacent surfaces to the sink are protected from water splashes (via splash guards or other means).

strategies.guideline: 4. Optimize staff workflow
strategies.strategy: 4.7. Care provider furniture can easily be moved to accommodate staff and patient needs.
served_outcomes: Adequate care for co-occurring med/psych conditions
Provision of appropriate psych care in the ED

strategies.guideline: 4. Optimize staff workflow
strategies.strategy: 4.8. There is a clearly defined zone for a patient observer, out of the way of key staff workflows.

strategies.guideline: 4. Optimize staff workflow
strategies.strategy: 4.9. Patient observer furniture can easily be moved to accommodate varying needs.
served_outcomes: Adequate care for co-occurring med/psych conditions
Provision of appropriate psych care in the ED

strategies.guideline: 4. Optimize staff workflow
strategies.strategy: 4.10. It is possible for a patient observer to sit within arms' length of the patient without blocking staff workflows.

strategies.guideline: 4. Optimize staff workflow
strategies.strategy: 4.11. The computer workstation (either fixed or mobile) is positionable such that staff can maintain a clear line of sight to the patient while using the workstation.

strategies.guideline: 4. Optimize staff workflow
strategies.strategy: 4.12. The patient bed and/or chair can be moved in and out of the room with ease.

strategies.guideline: 4. Optimize staff workflow
strategies.strategy: 4.13. The room door size, type, and location supports rapid, unobstructed entry into the room and to the patient bed and/or chair.

strategies.guideline: 4. Optimize staff workflow
strategies.strategy: 4.14. The vitals monitor is positioned to be conveniently visible and unobstructed to staff
served_outcomes: Adequate care for co-occurring med/psych conditions

strategies.guideline: 4. Optimize staff workflow
strategies.strategy: 4.15. The headwall is positioned and kept free of obstructions, providing adequate working clearance so staff can quickly reach and operate all receptacles, devices, and connections.
served_outcomes: Adequate care for co-occurring med/psych conditions

strategies.guideline: 4. Optimize staff workflow
strategies.strategy: 4.16. The headwall has all necessary medical gas outlets and electrical receptacles to power equipment.
served_outcomes: Adequate care for co-occurring med/psych conditions

strategies.guideline: 4. Optimize staff workflow
strategies.strategy: 4.17. Emergency supplies/supply storage are unobstructed, easily accessible, and immediately visible upon entering the room
served_outcomes: Adequate care for co-occurring med/psych conditions

strategies.guideline: 4. Optimize staff workflow
strategies.strategy: 4.18. Medical supplies/supply storage are unobstructed, easily accessible, and immediately visible upon entering the room
served_outcomes: Adequate care for co-occurring med/psych conditions

strategies.guideline: 4. Optimize staff workflow
strategies.strategy: 4.19 A sharps container is easily accessible to staff

strategies.guideline: 4. Optimize staff workflow
strategies.strategy: 4.20. Storage is easily accessible and not blocked by equipment or staff workflows

strategies.guideline: 4. Optimize staff workflow
strategies.strategy: 4.21. The computer workstation (whether fixed or mobile), is positioned (or can be positioned) such that it is easy to access and will not be blocked by mobile equipment or staff workflows.

strategies.guideline: 4. Optimize staff workflow
strategies.strategy: 4.22. The procedures workstation (whether fixed or mobile), is positioned (or can be positioned) such that it is easy to access and will not be blocked by mobile equipment or staff workflows

strategies.guideline: 4. Optimize staff workflow
strategies.strategy: 4.23. Opening and closing the door does not conflict with or obstruct workflows

strategies.guideline: 4. Optimize staff workflow
strategies.strategy: 4.24. Lighting allows for uniform, adequate illumination to perform examinations

strategies.guideline: 4. Optimize staff workflow
strategies.strategy: 4.25. Lighting allows for adjustability in brightness and/or directionality

strategies.guideline: 5. Improve safety and security for staff
strategies.strategy: 5.1. The door's design (e.g., size, hardware, operation, location) facilitates rapid staff escape and provides an unobstructed exit pathway.

strategies.guideline: 5. Improve safety and security for staff
strategies.strategy: 5.2. Patients cannot use the furniture in the room to trap staff members

strategies.guideline: 5. Improve safety and security for staff
strategies.strategy: 5.3. The room's layout allows staff to position themselves between the patient and the exit during monitoring and treatment to mitigate the potential of the patient trapping staff members in the room.

strategies.guideline: 5. Improve safety and security for staff
strategies.strategy: 5.4 The patient bed, if provided, can move to be accessed from all sides so two or more staff members can safely conduct de-escalation and/or restraint tasks.
served_outcomes: Provision of appropriate psych care in the ED

strategies.guideline: 5. Improve safety and security for staff
strategies.strategy: 5.5. The patient cannot access the contents of the sharps container.

strategies.guideline: 5. Improve safety and security for staff
strategies.strategy: 5.6. Patients cannot throw the furniture or use the furniture to harm staff members.

strategies.guideline: 6. Provide flexible and adaptable work environments
strategies.strategy: 6.1. The headwall can be closed, dismounted, or otherwise efficiently adapted to restrict patient access

strategies.guideline: 9. Provide opportunities for engaging patients and care partners
strategies.strategy: 9.1. Care partner furniture supports face-to-face conversation with staff members and the patient

strategies.guideline: 9. Provide opportunities for engaging patients and care partners
strategies.strategy: 9.2. The room's acoustic design supports private care partner-provider conversations right outside the room, protecting patients from heavy/difficult topics

strategies.guideline: 9. Provide opportunities for engaging patients and care partners
strategies.strategy: 9.3. Care partners have more than one resource to communicate with the ED nursing staff

strategies.guideline: 9. Provide opportunities for engaging patients and care partners
strategies.strategy: 9.4. The room's design comfortably accommodates more than one care partner

strategies.guideline: 9. Provide opportunities for engaging patients and care partners
strategies.strategy: 9.5. Care partner furniture does not impeded or disrupt staff workflows

strategies.guideline: 9. Provide opportunities for engaging patients and care partners
strategies.strategy: 9.6. The lighting design allows the family zone and patient zone to be controlled separately

strategies.guideline: 9. Provide opportunities for engaging patients and care partners
strategies.strategy: 9.7 There is a stable, cleanable work surface sized for shared activities between the patient and care providers

strategies.guideline: 9. Provide opportunities for engaging patients and care partners
strategies.strategy: 9.8. The room's design accommodates family members/care partners to sleep in the room

strategies.guideline: 10. Support patient and family dignity and comfort
strategies.strategy: 10.1. The room's interior design creates a warm and inviting ambiance

strategies.guideline: 10. Support patient and family dignity and comfort
strategies.strategy: 10.2. There are decorative elements in the room

strategies.guideline: 10. Support patient and family dignity and comfort
strategies.strategy: 10.3. The design incorporates lighting systems that can be programmed to adjust color, brightness, and/or timing to create specific moods or atmospheres

strategies.guideline: 10. Support patient and family dignity and comfort
strategies.strategy: 10.4. The design incorporates decorative biophilic elements

strategies.guideline: 10. Support patient and family dignity and comfort
strategies.strategy: 10.5. The design has windows (or simulated windows) offering views to natural elements

strategies.guideline: 10. Support patient and family dignity and comfort
strategies.strategy: 10.6. The design has windows with views to the outdoors

strategies.guideline: 10. Support patient and family dignity and comfort
strategies.strategy: 10.7. HVAC systems can be controlled and adjusted by staff to meet the needs of individuals with sensory sensitivities

strategies.guideline: 10. Support patient and family dignity and comfort
strategies.strategy: 10.8. The lighting design ensures adequate illumination while considering the needs of individuals with sensory sensitivities

strategies.guideline: 10. Support patient and family dignity and comfort
strategies.strategy: 10.9. Care partner and patient areas have diffuse lighting options for soft, uniform illumination

strategies.guideline: 10. Support patient and family dignity and comfort
strategies.strategy: 10.10 The design provides daylight access or Circadian lighting that mimics natural changes in lighting color and temperature throughout the day

strategies.guideline: 10. Support patient and family dignity and comfort
strategies.strategy: 10.11. Patient bed/chair is comfortable for extended periods of use

strategies.guideline: 10. Support patient and family dignity and comfort
strategies.strategy: 10.12. Patient bed/chair has height-adjustable head/foot sections

strategies.guideline: 10. Support patient and family dignity and comfort
strategies.strategy: 10.13. Additional seating furniture is comfortable for extended periods of use

strategies.guideline: 10. Support patient and family dignity and comfort
strategies.strategy: 10.14. The entertainment system is clearly visible from the patient bed/chair

strategies.guideline: 10. Support patient and family dignity and comfort
strategies.strategy: 10.15. The entertainment system is clearly visible from care partner seating

strategies.guideline: 10. Support patient and family dignity and comfort
strategies.strategy: 10.16.Furniture, fixtures, equipment, and finishes are constructed of impact-resistant materials that withstand abuse

strategies.guideline: 10. Support patient and family dignity and comfort
strategies.strategy: 10.17 Walls have guards or protection panels that prevent damage from moving furniture and equipment

strategies.guideline: 11. Create opportunities for auditory and visual privacy
strategies.strategy: 11.1. The design incorporates a white noise system to help mask external sounds and reduce noise disruptions

strategies.guideline: 11. Create opportunities for auditory and visual privacy
strategies.strategy: 11.2. Doors, windows, and walls are designed to minimize sound transmission and facilitate privacy and aural comfort

strategies.guideline: 11. Create opportunities for auditory and visual privacy
strategies.strategy: 11.3. There are blinds, shutters, or other privacy control measures on the windows facing the hallway to allow for adjustable levels of patient privacy

strategies.guideline: 11. Create opportunities for auditory and visual privacy
strategies.strategy: 11.4. There are blinds, shutters, or other privacy control measures on the windows facing the outdoors

strategies.guideline: 12. Provide patient choice and control over the environment
strategies.strategy: 12.1. Patients have more than one choice of place to sit, lie down, or stand

strategies.guideline: 12. Provide patient choice and control over the environment
strategies.strategy: 12.2. Patients can have control over the entertainment system

strategies.guideline: 12. Provide patient choice and control over the environment
strategies.strategy: 12.3. Patients can have control over the room's temperature

strategies.guideline: 12. Provide patient choice and control over the environment
strategies.strategy: 12.4. Patients can have control over the blinds and shutters on outdoor windows

strategies.guideline: 12. Provide patient choice and control over the environment
strategies.strategy: 12.5. Patients and care partners can control lighting

strategies.guideline: 12. Provide patient choice and control over the environment
strategies.strategy: 12.6. There is a dedicated, lockable storage area within the patient's line of sight for storing patient belongings.

strategies.guideline: 12. Provide patient choice and control over the environment
strategies.strategy: 12.7. The design allows for some level of visual customization by the patient

strategies.guideline: 13. Create opportunities for sensory-friendly support
strategies.strategy: 13.1. The room's decoration/aesthetic is appropriate for the full range of ages of patients that will be cared for in the room

strategies.guideline: 13. Create opportunities for sensory-friendly support
strategies.strategy: 13.2. The room's design incorporates opportunities for sensory experiences in the room

strategies.guideline: 13. Create opportunities for sensory-friendly support
strategies.strategy: 13.4. The design offers opportunities for positive patient distraction

strategies.guideline: 13. Create opportunities for sensory-friendly support
strategies.strategy: 13.5. The room's design incorporates opportunities for immersive experiences within the room

strategies.guideline: 13. Create opportunities for sensory-friendly support
strategies.strategy: 13.6. The entertainment system's content changes in real-time based on user inputs

design_alignment.note: User answers 'Successfully Implemented?' per strategy. Guideline importance pulled from guideline_prioritization override.

dat_report.title: Design Alignment Tool Report
dat_report.note: Per guideline: importance_weight = score for guideline's importance label (importance_scale). total_items = count strategies != 'Not Applicable'. yes = count 'Yes'. maximum_score = total_items * importance_weight. score_obtained = yes * importance_weight. alignment_score = score_obtained / maximum_score (percentage). Maybe/No count as 0.
